distractors.C: =D2+C2 adds in and out together, doesn't give the balance.
distractors.D: =F2-D2 references the wrong column.

questions.stem: The LAST step in solving a problem is to
questions.explanation: The standard problem-solving lifecycle: define the problem → analyse → design → implement → test → evaluate. Evaluation is the final step — judging whether the solution actually solved the original problem.

distractors.A: Testing comes BEFORE evaluation — you test to see if the solution works, then evaluate whether it solved the problem.
distractors.B: Specifying the design is an early step.
distractors.D: Developing the algorithm is a middle step (design phase).

questions.stem: Keyboards, speakers and CD drives are
questions.explanation: Peripheral devices are external devices connected to the system unit. The list mixes input (keyboard), output (speaker) and storage (CD drive) — what they all share is that they are peripheral.

distractors.B: Only CD drive is storage — keyboards and speakers are not.
distractors.C: Only the speaker is purely output — keyboard is input, CD drive is both.
distractors.D: Only the keyboard is purely input.

questions.stem: The Arithmetic/Logic Unit (ALU) is part of the
questions.explanation: The CPU contains two main parts: the Control Unit (CU) and the Arithmetic/Logic Unit (ALU). So the ALU is a sub-component of the CPU.

distractors.A: The CU is the OTHER component of the CPU, alongside the ALU — they are siblings, not parent/child.
distractors.B: ROM is memory, not part of the CPU.
distractors.D: RAM is main memory, separate from the CPU.

questions.stem: Which of the following is MOST suitable for printing building plans?
questions.explanation: A plotter is designed to draw very large, precise line drawings — ideal for architectural and engineering plans, maps, posters.

distractors.B: Laser printers are limited to standard page sizes (A4, A3) — too small for full-size building plans.
distractors.C: Dot-matrix is low-resolution and small-format — bad for detailed plans.
distractors.D: Thermal transfer is for receipts and labels — not large-format technical drawings.

questions.stem: Which of the following primary storage devices can be programmed only ONCE?
questions.explanation: PROM (Programmable Read-Only Memory) can be written to once — after that it's permanent. The "P" for Programmable distinguishes it from plain ROM (factory-set).

distractors.A: RAM can be written to many times — it's read/write memory.
distractors.C: EPROM is Erasable PROM — can be erased (with UV light) and reprogrammed.
distractors.D: EEPROM is Electrically Erasable PROM — can be erased and rewritten many times.

questions.stem: Laser beam technology is used by a
questions.explanation: Optical disks (CD, DVD, Blu-ray) are read and written using a laser beam. The laser detects pits and lands on the disk surface to encode data.

distractors.A: Floppy disks use magnetic technology, not laser.
distractors.C: Magnetic disks (HDD) use magnetisation of platters with a read/write head, not laser.
distractors.D: Magnetic tape uses magnetic encoding, not laser.

questions.stem: In computing, 'word wrap' refers to

options.B: placing text at the back of a graphic
options.C: the automatic carrying forward of text once the current line is filled
options.D: the user having to manually place the cursor on a new line when the previous one is filled

questions.explanation: Word wrap is the automatic feature that moves a word to the next line when the current line is full — the user does not have to press Enter at the end of each line.

distractors.A: That's "text wrapping around an image" (a layout feature) — different from word wrap.
distractors.B: "Behind text" image positioning is a layout choice, not word wrap.
distractors.D: That's the OPPOSITE of word wrap — manual line breaks were the norm before word wrap was invented.

questions.stem: While entering text in a word processing document, Michelle noticed that each new letter entered was replacing the previous one. The mode activated in this case is
questions.explanation: Type over (overwrite) mode replaces existing characters as you type. In Word, the Insert key on the keyboard toggles between Insert and Overtype modes.

distractors.B: Word wrap automatically moves text to the next line — has nothing to do with overwriting.
distractors.C: Delete removes characters — it doesn't replace them with new text.
distractors.D: Insert mode pushes existing text to the right — it's the OPPOSITE of what's described.

questions.stem: You need to enter the text "WEEKLY REPORT" to appear in the middle of the space used by the five columns below. Which of the following spreadsheet commands will enable this to be done?
questions.explanation: Merge and Centre combines selected cells into one large cell AND centres the text within that merged cell — exactly what's needed to display a heading across multiple columns.

distractors.A: Centring the text alone won't put it across multiple columns — it would just centre within a single cell.
distractors.B: Indenting moves text to the right but doesn't merge cells.
distractors.D: Merging the columns combines them but doesn't centre the text — you'd still need to centre afterwards. 'Merge and Centre' does both in one click.
